title: Tensions between the United States and Iran drive up crude oil and hit global markets
summary: Mutual attacks, the threat of closure of the Strait of Hormuz, and the war escalation send oil prices soaring and weaken the yen, which surpasses 162 units per dollar.

title: Fire at a bar in Bangkok leaves at least 28 dead; failures in emergency exits under investigation
summary: Thai authorities are investigating whether the emergency doors were blocked, while most of the bodies were found in the bathroom of the establishment.

title: Physiotherapist arrested for stabbing four people in the city of Saiki, Oita
summary: The 44-year-old suspect told police he did not care about the identity of his victims and was arrested for violating the weapons control law.

title: Japan's Supreme Court recognizes the standing of three citizens in the Henoko base lawsuit
summary: The historic decision sends the case back to lower courts and allows local litigants to legally challenge the relocation of the Futenma base.

title: Kabuki actor Nakamura Shozaburo investigated for alleged hit-and-run
summary: Tokyo police questioned the actor on suspicion of fleeing after a traffic accident in which no serious injuries were reported.

title: Government and opposition clash in the Diet over the deputy capital bill and extension of the session period
summary: The ruling party's proposal, seen as a tool for the Osaka metropolis, faces harsh criticism from the opposition demanding its modification.

title: Revision of the Imperial House Law begins in the Diet with the opposition demanding changes
summary: The government proposal to allow male succession from the former imperial house is called 'deceptive' by the opposition, which presents its own amendments.

title: Japan's government and opposition close in on a refundable tax credit for low-income households
summary: The new scheme, which would also consider people with illnesses or disabilities, raises concern among mayors over the administrative burden it will impose on municipalities.

title: Zelensky announces a cabinet reshuffle as Ukraine intensifies attacks on Russian military infrastructure
summary: The prime minister would be replaced by the current ambassador to the United States, while Ukrainian forces strike refineries and ships in the Sea of Azov.

title: Extreme heat alert in Japan with temperatures exceeding 38 degrees Celsius in several regions
summary: Authorities issue heatstroke alerts in 14 prefectures, especially in the west of the country, where the highest temperatures are expected along with a high risk for farmers.

title: Justice orders reopening of investigation against politician Tachibana for threats to a Hyogo assemblyman
summary: A fiscal review committee deemed the initial decision not to prosecute Tachibana for his threats against a provincial assemblyman in the context of a whistleblowing case to be improper.

title: Data leak in LINE: more than 6 million game user IDs mistakenly sent to an external server
summary: The company confirmed that the leaked information does not include passwords or payment data, and that no fraudulent use of the identifiers has been detected so far.

title: European Union proposes strict regulation of social media and screen use for minors
summary: The plan presented by Ursula von der Leyen will ban the use of smartphones for children under 3 and require mandatory parental supervision up to age 13.

title: Liberal Democratic Party urges increase in female representation in upcoming Upper House elections
summary: A report from the ruling party proposes prioritizing women on proportional representation lists and requests its supporting organizations to nominate more female candidates.

title: Japan enacts law to create the Disaster Management Agency, set to begin operations in November
summary: The new agency will centralize the government's response to emergencies and natural disasters, establishing regional coordination bases.

title: Turkish citizen dies in police custody at Takao police station, Tokyo
summary: The Metropolitan Police Department is investigating the death as a possible natural causes death, reviewing the procedures followed during his detention.

title: Cyberattack on Nichirei company affects frozen product shipping system
summary: Unauthorized access to its systems caused partial disruptions in food distribution, although the company assures it is working to restore service.

title: Actor Sam Neill, famous for his role as Dr. Alan Grant in 'Jurassic Park', dies at 78
summary: The New Zealand actor, who also starred in films like 'The Hunt for Red October', died surrounded by his family.

title: The messenger boy of Kyoto's Gion Festival performs the historic horse-mounted shrine visit ceremony
summary: The Shasan-no-gi ceremony marks the official start of the Gion Matsuri festivities, where a local boy, dressed as a divine messenger, officiates as a representative of the city.

title: 67-year-old man arrested for fatal hit-and-run of a woman in Kyoto on murder charges
summary: Prefectural police applied homicide charges to the suspect, who allegedly fled after running over the victim, and the possible involvement of two other vehicles is being investigated.
